title: Marbella Boat Rental Weekend: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
For a memorable weekend on the Costa del Sol, hiring a boat in Marbella from Puerto Banús gives you access to the Mediterranean at prices from €250 for a half-day motorboat up to €3,500 for a full-day crewed yacht. BoatHire24 makes it straightforward to search available vessels across the port.

Where to set off in Marbella

Your weekend afloat starts from one of several well-equipped marinas. Puerto Banús is the glamorous hub, packed with superyachts and waterside restaurants. For a quieter departure, head to Marbella Marina (also known as Puerto Deportivo de Marbella) near the old town. Playa de la Fontanilla offers a simple launch point for smaller craft, while Cabopino Marina just east of Marbella provides access to the Cabopino dunes and quieter waters. Further out, the Bay of Estepona and the anchorage at Calahonda are popular short-run destinations.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

A weekend rental typically ranges from €250 for a small motorboat to €3,500 for a fully crewed superyacht for a full day. Prices vary by season, boat size and whether you hire a skipper. You can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to see exactly what is on offer.

  • Shared sunset tour: from €60 per person for a 2-hour trip from Puerto Banús.
  • Small motorboat (half-day without skipper): from €250 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day: from €1,200 for up to 12 guests, fuel and crew sometimes included.
  • Full-day crewed yacht (e.g. 50ft Azimut): from €2,500 for up to 8 guests.

Which boat to rent for your Marbella weekend

  1. Rib or speedboat, best for a fast tour along the coast to Marbella’s coves; ideal for 4, 6 guests; from €350 half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker Predator 72), for a luxurious day out with a flybridge and deck space; fits up to 8; from €1,500 half-day; see this motor yacht for full specs.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 46), stable and spacious, great for a group of friends or families; sleeps up to 12; from €1,200 full-day.
  4. Sailboat (e.g. Dufour 40), for a quieter, more traditional weekend; 6, 8 guests; from €600 full-day.
  5. Crewed superyacht (e.g. Maiora 80), all-inclusive luxury with skipper and crew; up to 10 guests; from €3,500 full-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

For boats over 8 metres (26 ft) or with an engine power above 15 hp, you generally need a Spanish recreational boating licence, but some smaller boats can be rented without a licence. The operator will confirm exact requirements. Many renters choose a licensed skipper instead, which is often compulsory for larger yachts.

For an unforgettable weekend, plan your trip between June and September when the Levante wind is calmest. Motor east from Cabopino to the Bay of Calahonda (about 5 nautical miles) for a calm swim spot before the afternoon sea breeze picks up.

At a glance

  • Price range: €250, €3,500
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4h) or full-day (8h)
  • Maximum capacity: 6, 12 guests (depending on boat)
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departure points: Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I take a boat from Marbella to Gibraltar for the day?

It is possible but requires crossing international waters and calling ahead to clear customs; most weekend renters prefer staying within the Costa del Sol due to time constraints.

What is included in the rental price?

Typically you get the boat, life jackets, basic safety equipment, and sometimes fuel for the first hour; crewed charters often include a skipper, host and light refreshments.

Is it safe to anchor near Marbella’s beaches?

Yes, but avoid protected areas; the best anchorages are off Cabopino and Calahonda where the seabed is sandy and you are far from jet ski lanes.

Do I need to book well in advance for a summer weekend?

Yes, especially for popular models like the Sunseeker or a Lagoon catamaran; booking by May is wise for July or August weekends in 2026.

A weekend boat rental in Marbella offers flexible access to the Mediterranean’s finest coves, from the glamour of Puerto Banús to the quiet of Calahonda. Whether you choose a self-drive speedboat or a crewed superyacht, BoatHire24 helps you find and book the right vessel. If you own a boat, you can list a boat for free on the marketplace. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
